items.title: The greyhound races Tasmanian authorities don't want you to see

children.content: If you watched greyhound race videos on Tasracing's website, you would likely never know that dozens of dogs were injured or killed during events last year.

avatar.alt: A woman smiling at the camera.

items.title: Shyanne Lee-Tatnell's accused murderer likely to face trial next year

children.content: A man who has pleaded not guilty to murdering 14-year-old Tasmanian teenager Shyanne Lee-Tatnell in 2023 will likely stand trial early next year.

image.altText: A young blonde girl smiles at the camera

items.title: Plans for DarkLab Transformer artwork ditched amid rising costs

children.content: The group behind Tasmania's Dark Mofo festival has scrapped plans to develop a "destination public artwork" in the state's south-east due to escalating costs.

image.altText: Mirrored buildings on the side of a hill

items.title: 'They're often not heard': Hip hop helping kids find words for the feelings

children.content: A Tasmanian youth outreach program is providing free hip hop writing workshops for young people, which experts say could be "life-changing".

gallery.altText: Kieran's hand as he writes on white paper, resting on a brown table. He wears a purple hoodie and his hand has tattoos.

items.title: Aboriginal activist takes on logging company staff during trespass court case

children.content: An Aboriginal activist has interrogated forestry company staff over their knowledge of cultural heritage as part of her defence against a trespass charge.

image.altText: Ruth Langford, wearing a white jacket, standing in front of the Hobart Magistrates Court smiling

items.title: The ongoing cost of medical misogyny for women

children.content: Australia has come a long way when it comes to women's healthcare, but experts say medical misogyny is still very much alive — and it's not just attitudes that are the problem.

image.altText: A woman lies on the couch with one hand on her head and another on her lower abdomen

items.title: Tougher penalties, but no gun limit under Tasmania's proposed new laws

children.content: New gun laws, which create tougher penalties for the theft and possession of stolen firearms, but do not include gun caps, have been unveiled by the Tasmanian government, asking for public feedback.

gallery.altText: Collection of seized and stolen firearms

thumbnail.altText: Collection of seized and stolen firearms

items.title: The Trump era echoes the messy truth behind the founding of America

children.content: US Independence Day was never the simple morality tale Americans often tell themselves. Under Donald Trump the debate over its meaning is more politically charged than ever under.

articles.title: Are America's founding principles being challenged?

children.content: The United States is celebrating its 250th anniversary today. Let's take a closer look at the document that birthed the nation and where things stand now.

image.altText: A painting that depicts five men drafting the Declaration of Independence

items.title: Investors consider class action over super scam

children.content: Investors who lost out in a superannuation scam are considering a potential class action.

image.altText: A middle-aged woman standing in front of a caravan

items.title: 'The world lost him': Crowds gather in Iran to farewell body of assassinated supreme leader

children.content: Millions of Iranians have gathered in Tehran as the country begins a week of funeral ceremonies for former supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

image.altText: Crowds wearing black and waving flags gather outside a large building, decorated with a large banner of Ali Khamenei.

items.title: For 60 years, Play School has never broken this rule

children.content: Australian Story is given rare behind-the-scenes access to Play School as it celebrates 60 years on air. An intimate look at the creative process of the iconic children’s TV show that’s still guided by one golden rule.

items.title: Taylor Swift's wedding a lesson in 'media mastery'

children.content: After mounting speculation, Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have officially tied the knot. One expert says what we know, and don't know, about the wedding is a lesson in "media mastery".

image.altText: A man in a black shirt holds a woman wearing a black and white dress in a warm embrace in a flower-filled garden

items.title: Bird flu threatens to devastate seabird population on Great Barrier Reef

children.content: A vulnerable colony of red-tailed tropic birds is being monitored for the deadly H5 bird flu, with fears the impact on Australian native bird populations could be significant.

image.altText: A fluffy white bird with a black beak

items.title: How NAIDOC grew from a one-day protest to a week-long celebration

children.content: Traditionally, NAIDOC Week was celebrated by First Nations people, but it has become an invitation for non-Indigenous Australians to get involved.

gallery.altText: A montage of images of NAIDOC protests and image of Day of Mourning with Indigenous leaders in 1938

children.content: Surprise-packets Paraguay take on hot-favourites France, Morocco marches into World Cup quarterfinals with 3-0 win over Canada. Follow live.

image.altText: France and Paraguay players scuffle

items.title: 'Dismantled from the inside out': Finding connection after child loss

children.content: After Kimberly Stevens lost her teenage son she spent years building an app to help other bereaved parents, and she is now taking its findings to an international audience.

image.altText: A selfie of a woman and young teenage boy smiling at the camera

items.title: App fatigue can 'warp' dating but there's an antidote

children.content: Dating apps promised more choice than ever, but experts say the endless swiping and split-second decisions may be leaving users more exhausted, and less connected, than before. But deleting them may not be the only solution.

children.content: Will those primary school spelling sheets be enough to pull you a pass?

items.title: The desert kids making classic hard rock cool again

children.content: Inspired by AC/DC and raised in a remote NT community, these young cousins have become one of Australia's most unlikely hard rock bands.

image.altText: Six members of Mulga Bore Hard Rock with black-and-white face paint pose for a group portrait in a studio setting

items.title: The spearfisherman who rediscovered a shipwreck

children.content: The startling noise of metal on metal was unusual on a coral reef and unlocked a nearly century-old mystery of a tragic maritime disaster, sunken treasure and a peculiar, inscribed turtle shell.

items.title: Japan releases LGBTQIA+ education plan but some are not happy

children.content: The first national plan of its kind is broadly criticised by gay rights proponents and legal experts for supporting the status quo.

image.altText: Two women give the peace sign in a selfie with a large mountain in the background

items.title: Sydney Trains female apprentices step up amid 94pc gender skew

children.content: Nicola Huggins and Ariana Gackowski are learning the wiring, welding and hand tool skills required to one day repair the trains Sydneysiders travel on every day.

items.title: Broken back prompts apprentice electrician to chase footy career

items.title: Two Sydney Mardi Gras board members removed after months of disputes

children.content: Damien Nguyen and Luna Choo from Pride in Protest were voted off the Sydney Gay and Lesbian Mardi Gras board by a 70 per cent majority.

items.title: Government rejects all UN recommendations on LGBTQIA+ rights

children.content: The Albanese government has accepted the lowest number of recommendations made by the UN Human Rights Council in 20 years, with none protecting the LGBTQIA+ community.
